name: Yan Jian Rou (Chili Pork)
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Rice wine or dry sherry
1 tsp Sichuan peppercorns, roasted and crushed
recipeInstructions:
1. CUT THE PORK into 3-inch thin slices and combine with the soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice wine, salt, and sesame oil.
2. Cut the chiles in half lengthwise and seed them.
3. Heat a wok or large saute pan until it is hot. Add 1½ tablespoons of peanut oil and the pork, and stir-fry for 1 minute. Remove the pork with a slotted spoon.
4. Reheat the wok and add the remaining oil. When it is very hot, add the fresh chiles, garlic, chili powder or cayenne, and peppercorns, and stir-fry for 30 seconds.
5. Add the water, 1 tablespoon rice wine, soy sauce, and sugar, and cook for another 30 seconds.
6. Return the meat to the wok and heat through. Give the mixture several good stirs.
7. Turn off the heat and serve on a platter. Serves 4 as part of a Chinese meal or 2 as a single dish.
